attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as 45 (adult) — the source note gives no age; lichen planus typically presents in mid-adulthood. Maintainer/clinician to confirm on review.
Sex inferred as female — the source note gives no sex; cutaneous lichen planus has a slight female predominance. Maintainer/clinician to confirm on review.
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-03-00202 — SEQ derived from source AMS-022; ingest --reseq will assign the real globally-unique sequence in the SPEC-DERM-03 bucket.
Difficulty classified red_herring_laden (03): the patient's fear of a contagious parasite plus alarming excoriation are red herrings distracting from a benign autoimmune dermatosis.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person home visit with a full dermatological exam and clinician-measured vitals. Confirmatory exam findings (Wickham striae, flat-topped violaceous morphology, absence of burrows, no cellulitis signs) and vitals (BP 128/78, HR 88, Temp 36.9, RR 16, SpO2 100%) are sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; patient-observable data (visible rash appearance, self-reported no-fever, body weight) placed in 01.objective_data_offered as unverified.

No numeric drug doses were stated in the source note (betamethasone dipropionate ointment and promethazine named without strength/dose). Per protocol no dose was synthesised; dose_route_frequency records route/timing only and defers the numeric dose to the prescriber. No paediatric dosing involved (adult).

Baseline tier set T2 (prompt GP/telehealth-manageable) against the source's 'urgent after-hours' framing — honest re-tiering of a benign, afebrile, localised dermatosis; escalation edges cover the true red flags (secondary infection T3/T4, mucosal spread T2).

All medical codes (SNOMED CT-AU, ICD-10-AM) are unverified candidates pending terminology-server receipts. AMT/PBS drug codes were NOT assigned (uncertain) rather than guessing a plausible-but-wrong code.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
